requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must contribute sufficient employee deferrals to capture full employer matching contribution, effectively receiving tax-free compensation and guaranteed investment return through employer retirement plan match
criteria: Must be eligible for employer matching contributions under plan's vesting and eligibility requirements
Common match formulas: 50% or 100% of employee deferrals up to 3-6% of compensation
Contribute at least enough to receive maximum employer match (typically 6% of salary)
Match typically requires employee deferral; no deferral means no match regardless of eligibility
Understand vesting schedule: immediate, cliff (e.g., 3 years), or graded (e.g., 20% per year over 6 years)
Safe harbor plans provide immediate vesting and avoid most nondiscrimination testing
Timing matters: some plans require employment on last day of year; others use pay period matching
Consider impact of bonus compensation on match calculation depending on plan definition of compensation

employer_match_optimization.free_money: Employer match is immediate guaranteed return on employee contributions (50-100% ROI)
employer_match_optimization.tax_advantaged: Match contributions tax-free when received; taxed as ordinary income when distributed
employer_match_optimization.vesting_schedule: Match may vest over time; understand schedule before job change to maximize vested balance
employer_match_optimization.safe_harbor_match: Safe harbor 401(k) plans provide immediate 100% vesting and nondiscrimination relief
employer_match_optimization.true_up_contributions: Some plans provide annual true-up ensuring full match even with irregular contribution timing
employer_match_optimization.combined_limits: Employee deferrals + employer match + other contributions limited to $70,000 (2025)

restrictions: Must contribute to receive match; employer typically won't match if employee doesn't defer
Matching formula varies by plan; review SPD to understand exact calculation and maximization strategy
Vesting cliff can result in forfeiture if leave employer before vesting; understand schedule before job changes
Timing of contributions affects match if no true-up provision; front-loading may forfeit later match opportunities
Highly compensated employees may face matching contribution restrictions due to nondiscrimination testing
Employer match counts toward overall plan contribution limit ($70,000 for 2025)
Match typically only on elective deferrals, not on after-tax contributions or other plan contributions
Job change before vesting complete may forfeit unvested match; coordinate timing with vesting schedule
